target.title: Draft: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.
- Adds a small before-tool-call fact adapter so future runtime wiring can build the same audit event shape from explicit boundary facts without changing execution behavior.

## Scope

This PR remains non-enforcing and observe-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution. The new before-tool-call adapter only builds an audit event object and exposes an explicit opt-in config predicate for future wiring.

## Validation

Previously validated locally against `upstream/main` before the before-tool-call adapter commits:

```bash
pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts
pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
pnpm docs:list | grep -n "security/security-matrix.md"
```

Previous results:

- 25 focused tests passed
- changed-lanes reported `core=true`, `coreTests=true`, `docs=true`, `tooling=true`
- oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors
- docs formatting reported clean
- git whitespace check reported clean
- docs index included `security/security-matrix.md`

Latest branch updates were made through GitHub file writes from this review session. Local validation should be rerun before marking ready for review.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The Security Matrix evaluator classifies runtime tool facts using actor, influence source, capability, approval state, and operator policy. Actor identity is modeled separately from content influence, and policy decisions are produced from the runtime fact set. The latest branch also adds a before-tool-call fact adapter that creates the same audit event shape from explicit boundary facts but does not publish or enforce it.

Observed result from prior local validation: The evaluator tests passed and covered the expected policy matrix behavior, including externally influenced restricted classes, externally influenced state-changing classes, externally influenced read/network/browser/memory-read classes, actor/source separation, approval handling, operator-policy denial, custom-policy weakening guards, concrete tool-id capability mapping, and audit-event object creation.

What is still not tested in this PR: live runtime hook emission, runtime enforcement, config schema adoption, CLI display, plugin UI, and live tool execution. Those remain intentionally out of scope until the runtime path can provide real provenance facts and wire the adapter behind an explicit opt-in.

## AI assistance

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated code and understand the model, evaluator, tests, documentation, and before-tool-call adapter.
